_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The workspace operates as a freight forwarder and logistics coordinator, sitting between the consignor (livestock keeper or exporter in Northern Ireland or Great Britain) and the receiving premises in Scotland (abattoir, farm, or assembly centre). In the bluetongue certification chain, the workspace does not itself hold official veterinary certification authority — that role belongs to the Authorised Veterinary Practitioner (aPVP) who issues the standard GB export certificate through DAERA electronic systems (eAVI for cattle, eSAVI for sheep and goats) — but the workspace is operationally responsible for ensuring that the *full documentation pack* travels with the animals and is correct at the time of movement.

Specifically, DAERA requires that exporters complete both (i) the standard GB certificate generated via DAERA systems and (ii) the Scotland-specific supplemental certificate, with the supplemental certificate cross-referenced to the standard certificate number so the two are linked. The workspace, as the entity managing the consignment, must verify that both documents are present and correctly completed before authorising loading. Where the workspace arranges transport on behalf of the consignor, it also bears operational responsibility for the transport conditions: animals must not be unloaded between origin and destination except at scheduled stops (motorway service stations, official lay-bys, lorry parks or similar places).

From 1 June 2026, the workspace must additionally verify that consignments entering the Scottish high-risk control period are supported by the required general licence (EXD608 or equivalent, published by Scottish Government) or, for movements requiring a specific licence, by an individual APHA-issued licence applied for in advance. For slaughter movements, the workspace must confirm that the destination abattoir is designated for receiving livestock from bluetongue control zones before issuing shipping instructions.

**Role placement requiring legal confirmation:** Whether the workspace, as freight forwarder, bears any direct legal liability under Northern Ireland or Scottish animal health legislation for documentation errors (as distinct from the consignor and certifying vet) should be confirmed with legal counsel, as the statutory duty to produce the supplemental certificate rests with the exporter/keeper, not the carrier.

*Source: Moving cattle, sheep and goats to Scotland while Scotland is Bluetongue Free, DAERA, 2026. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/publications/moving-cattle-sheep-and-goats-scotland-while-scotland-bluetongue-free; Live Animals Exports, DAERA. https://www.daera-ni.gov.uk/services/live-animals-exports*
